name: Timor-Leste
avoid: December–April rains can wash out mountain and far-east roads.
Boat crossings to Ataúro and Jaco are weather dependent; keep one flexible buffer day.
Carry USD cash outside Dili — ATMs and card acceptance are limited in Baucau, Maubisse and Tutuala.
stopoverNote: Most travellers start and finish in Dili because Presidente Nicolau Lobato International Airport has the broadest connections; keep Dili as your transport anchor for Ataúro ferries and overland trips east or into the highlands.

cities.name: Dili
cities.notes: Capital base for Cristo Rei, the Timorese Resistance Archive & Museum, Areia Branca beach and practical ferry departures to Ataúro Island.

cities.name: Baucau
cities.notes: Historic east-coast town with colonial architecture, a photogenic old market, and the best staging point for long road trips toward Tutuala and Jaco Island.

cities.name: Ataúro Island
cities.notes: Coral Triangle island escape for reef diving, village boat rides, Boneca handicrafts and slow beach days around Beloi and Adara.

cities.name: Maubisse
cities.notes: Cool highland base for Tatamailau panoramas, colonial pousada views, coffee landscapes and community stays around Hakmatek and Hatu-Builico.

itinerary.sim: Telemor or Telkomcel prepaid SIMs are the practical choice in Timor-Leste; expect roughly ₹415–₹830 for basic 7–15 day data packs, and buy in Dili before heading to Ataúro, Baucau or Maubisse where top-up options are thinner.

Dili.name: Dili
Dili.note: Use Dili for your arrival buffer, the core historical sights and one beach day; it is also the easiest place to organize taxis, microlets and ferry tickets before going elsewhere.

days.theme: Arrival, resistance history and Dili waterfront

activities.name: Presidente Nicolau Lobato Airport taxi into central Dili
activities.tip: Most drivers quote in USD cash, so having small notes ready speeds things up after arrival.

activities.name: Timorese Resistance Archive & Museum
activities.tip: The foreign visitor ticket is about US$5 and gives the best crash course on the independence struggle.

activities.name: Tais Market browsing for handwoven textiles
Largo de Lecidere seafront sunset walk

days.theme: Cristo Rei steps, Areia Branca and beachside Dili

activities.name: Taxi from central Dili to Cristo Rei trailhead
Cristo Rei of Dili statue climb and viewpoints
activities.tip: Go at sunrise or late afternoon — there is no official entrance fee, just the heat and the stair climb.

activities.name: Dolok Oan / Back Beach swim below Cristo Rei
Return by taxi or beachside microlet toward Areia Branca

days.theme: Microlet city hop, Santa Cruz history and final planning day

activities.name: Microlet rides between Lecidere, Becora and central Dili
activities.tip: Microlets are the cheapest city transport; ask locals which route number is currently running because signage is inconsistent.

activities.name: Santa Cruz Cemetery historical visit
Motael Church and waterfront quarter stroll
Advance ferry or boat arrangements for Ataúro departure

Ataúro Island.name: Ataúro Island
Ataúro Island.note: Ataúro is best with two nights if you want both reef time and an unhurried village day; wind or swell can reshuffle ferry schedules, so avoid making same-day international flight connections from here.

days.theme: Beloi arrival, village shoreline and Boneca handicrafts

activities.name: Dragon Star fast boat from Dili to Beloi
activities.tip: The cheaper fallback is the slower public angguna/cargo-style boat at about ₹498–₹830 one way, but departure times can shift.

activities.name: Beloi village and beachfront orientation walk
Boneca de Ataúro cooperative workshop visit
activities.tip: Entry is usually informal; budget for a small handicraft purchase if you want to support the women’s co-op.

activities.name: Short angguna ride from Beloi toward Adara coast for sunset snorkel access

days.theme: Coral Triangle dive day or reef snorkelling escape

activities.name: Atauro Dive Resort or Compass Diving single-tank dive with equipment
activities.tip: Expect roughly US$50–70 depending on boat fuel and whether you book one dive or a multi-dive package.

activities.name: Guided snorkel boat to outer reef sites
Mount Manucoco village hike with local guide

Baucau.name: Baucau
Baucau.note: One full day covers Baucau town, but two works better if you want to push farther east to Tutuala and Jaco Island without rushing the return to Dili.

days.theme: Colonial Baucau, old market and east-coast views

activities.name: Shared minibus from Dili to Baucau
activities.tip: Private cars are faster but much pricier; the shared route usually takes 3–4 hours with roadside stops.

activities.name: Pousada de Baucau hilltop terrace and historic quarter walk
Mercado Municipal de Baucau and old Portuguese lanes
Wataboo Beach late-afternoon stop

days.theme: Tutuala road trip and sacred Jaco Island extension

activities.name: Early private car or shared overland ride from Baucau to Tutuala / Valu Sere
activities.tip: This is a long eastbound day, so start before sunrise or plan to overnight near Tutuala if you prefer a slower pace.

activities.name: Jaco Island local fisherman boat from Valu Sere
activities.tip: The standard crossing is about US$10 round-trip; Jaco is sacred, so day visits only and no overnight stays.

activities.name: Jaco Island beach and snorkelling time
Ili Kere Kere sea cave lookout near Tutuala

Maubisse.name: Maubisse
Maubisse.note: The highlands are all about cooler air, views and slow travel; keep two days if you want both Maubisse town and a Tatamailau/Ramelau sunrise mission from nearby Hatu-Builico.

days.theme: Highland arrival, market streets and pousada views

activities.name: Shared taxi or minibus from Dili to Maubisse
Pousada de Maubisse viewpoint and colonial grounds
Mercado de Maubisse walk for highland produce and coffee
Hakmatek Tourism sacred-house and valley viewpoint visit

days.theme: Tatamailau sunrise mission and coffee-country finale

activities.name: Pre-dawn drive to Hatu-Builico trailhead for Mount Ramelau / Tatamailau
Mount Ramelau local guide and trail support
activities.tip: Guide fees vary by group size; dry-season weekends are the easiest time to find local drivers and guides.

activities.name: Pousada Alecrim lunch stop in Hatu-Builico
Maubisse coffee farm tasting with local host

connections.method: Dragon Star fast boat to Beloi, with the slower public angguna/cargo-style boat as the budget backup when seas are calm
Return fast boat or public boat back to Dili port before continuing overland
Shared minibus or hired car along the north coast highway
Best done via a private car back through Dili and up into the highlands, though budget travellers normally split the route across two shared rides
connections.cost: ₹1,245–₹2,490 pp shared total or ₹10,790–₹14,110 per car

connections.method: Morning shared taxi or minibus downhill to the capital

extras: May to November is the sweet spot: roads are drier, visibility is better for Ataúro diving and the Jaco crossing is calmer.
Timor-Leste runs on USD cash; convert rough prices to INR only for planning and carry small bills for taxis, boats and museum tickets.
If you only have 5–6 days, keep Dili + Ataúro + one of either Baucau or Maubisse instead of trying to fit all four bases.
Jaco Island is culturally sacred to the Fataluku people, so respect local guidance, take your rubbish out and do not plan an overnight stay.
